project_title.value: Recovery Forecasting & Performance Optimization: A Wearable Data Decision Engine

citations.content: Recovery Forecasting & Performance Optimization: A Wearable Data Decision Engine

system_core_question.value: given everything your body has communicated through your Oura Ring overnight, what is the highest-ROI training decision for tomorrow?

base_formula_description.value: A weighted composite of normalized daily signals expressed as Z-scores against a rolling 28-day personal baseline.

citations.content: A weighted composite of normalized daily signals expressed as Z-scores against a rolling 28-day personal baseline.

description.value: Z-score of today's 7-day LnRMSSD rolling mean vs. 28-day baseline

citations.content: Z-score of today's 7-day LnRMSSD rolling mean vs. 28-day baseline

description.value: Inverted Z-score of nocturnal RHR vs. 28-day baseline (lower is better)

citations.content: Inverted Z-score of nocturnal RHR vs. 28-day baseline (lower is better)

description.value: Z-score of sleep score vs. personal baseline

citations.content: Z-score of sleep score vs. personal baseline

description.value: EWMA ACWR normalized to personal baseline

what_it_measures.value: Beat-to-beat variation in R-R intervals. A non-invasive proxy for ANS balance.

citations.content: Beat-to-beat variation in R-R intervals. A non-invasive proxy for ANS balance.

physiological_basis.value: Higher parasympathetic tone at rest indicates the organism is well-recovered. Lower RMSSD indicates sympathetic dominance.

citations.content: Higher parasympathetic tone at rest indicates the organism is well-recovered. Lower RMSSD indicates sympathetic dominance.
Higher parasympathetic tone at rest indicates the organism is well-recovered. Lower RMSSD indicates sympathetic dominance.
Higher parasympathetic tone at rest indicates the organism is well-recovered. Lower RMSSD indicates sympathetic dominance.

reliability.value: Day-to-day variability is inherent and expected. Single reading is often unreliable; 7-day rolling average is correct.

citations.content: Day-to-day variability is inherent and expected. Single reading is often unreliable; 7-day rolling average is correct.
Day-to-day variability is inherent and expected. Single reading is often unreliable; 7-day rolling average is correct.
Day-to-day variability is inherent and expected. Single reading is often unreliable; 7-day rolling average is correct.

sensitivity.value: Responds to all stressors indiscriminately (training load, sleep deprivation, alcohol, psychological stress).

citations.content: Responds to all stressors indiscriminately (training load, sleep deprivation, alcohol, psychological stress).
Responds to all stressors indiscriminately (training load, sleep deprivation, alcohol, psychological stress).

interpretation.value: Operationally meaningful thresholds for training modification

citations.content: operationally meaningful thresholds for training modification

next_day_training_relevance.value: High. The highest-value signal for day-to-day training decisions (when used as 7-day rolling average).

citations.content: High. The highest-value signal for day-to-day training decisions (when used as 7-day rolling average).
High. The highest-value signal for day-to-day training decisions (when used as 7-day rolling average).

evidence_summary.value: Confirmed as primary field-based monitoring metric (Esco et al. 2025). Oura accuracy validated (CCC = 0.97-0.99).

citations.content: Confirmed as primary field-based monitoring metric (Esco et al. 2025). Oura accuracy validated (CCC = 0.97-0.99).
Confirmed as primary field-based monitoring metric (Esco et al. 2025). Oura accuracy validated (CCC = 0.97-0.99).

important_notes.value: Parasympathetic Saturation: HRV can paradoxically decrease during heavy volume blocks even as parasympathetic activity increases.

citations.content: Parasympathetic Saturation: HRV can paradoxically decrease during heavy volume blocks even as parasympathetic activity increases.
Parasympathetic Saturation: HRV can paradoxically decrease during heavy volume blocks even as parasympathetic activity increases.

what_it_measures.value: Heart's intrinsic rate at rest. Balance of sympathetic and parasympathetic input.

citations.content: Heart's intrinsic rate at rest. Balance of sympathetic and parasympathetic input.
Heart's intrinsic rate at rest. Balance of sympathetic and parasympathetic input.

physiological_basis.value: Elevated RHR indicates sympathetic dominance/incomplete recovery.

citations.content: Elevated RHR indicates sympathetic dominance/incomplete recovery.

reliability.value: More stable and less sensitive than HRV. Changes likely reflect real physiological shifts.

citations.content: More stable and less sensitive than HRV. Changes likely reflect real physiological shifts.
More stable and less sensitive than HRV. Changes likely reflect real physiological shifts.

sensitivity.value: Responds more slowly than HRV (peaks 24-48 hours post-session). Confounders: alcohol, illness, dehydration.

citations.content: Responds more slowly than HRV (peaks 24-48 hours post-session). Confounders: alcohol, illness, dehydration.
Responds more slowly than HRV (peaks 24-48 hours post-session). Confounders: alcohol, illness, dehydration.

parentBlock.content:
| RHR Elevation Above Baseline   | Interpretation                              |
|-|-|
| 1-2 bpm                        | Within day-to-day noise; not actionable     |
| 3-4 bpm                        | Borderline; monitor alongside HRV and sleep |

| RHR Elevation Above Baseline   | Interpretation                              |
|-|-|
| 1-2 bpm                        | Within day-to-day noise; not actionable     |
| 3-4 bpm                        | Borderline; monitor alongside HRV and sleep |

| RHR Elevation Above Baseline   | Interpretation                                           |
|-|-|
| 5+ bpm                         | Actionable signal-reduce load or rest                    |
| 7-10+ bpm                      | Strong signal-consider illness, significant overreaching |
| Sustained elevation >2 weeks   | Evaluate for OTS; medical assessment warranted           |

interpretation.value: Actionable signal - reduce load or rest

physiological_basis.value: Deep sleep triggers GH release for muscle synthesis. Inadequate sleep elevates cortisol.

citations.content: Deep sleep triggers GH release for muscle synthesis. Inadequate sleep elevates cortisol.

reliability.value: Oura total sleep time accurate within ~30 min. Sleep staging less precise (Cohen's K = 0.65).

citations.content: Oura total sleep time accurate within ~30 min. Sleep staging less precise (Cohen's K = 0.65).
Oura total sleep time accurate within ~30 min. Sleep staging less precise (Cohen's K = 0.65).

sensitivity.value: Sensitive to training load and stress. Early sleep loss reduces N3; early alarm reduces REM.

citations.content: Sensitive to training load and stress. Early sleep loss reduces N3; early alarm reduces REM.
Sensitive to training load and stress. Early sleep loss reduces N3; early alarm reduces REM.

next_day_training_relevance.value: High. Total sleep <6 hours is a hard RED flag.

citations.content: High. Total sleep <6 hours is a hard RED flag.

important_notes.value: Sleep debt accumulates; perception does not track impairment.

citations.content: Sleep debt accumulates; perception does not track impairment.
